title: carlina vulgaris subsp. acanthophylla
intro: Carlina vulgaris subsp. acanthophylla is a plant species, also known as the lesser thistle, which belongs to the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as possessing antioxidant and antitumor activities. Traditional herbal preparations of Carlina vulgaris subsp. acanthophylla include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues and support overall well-being.
